checklist.description: Attempt messages show identity or activation validation behavior before the final answer (for example LeGreffier activation checks, bot identity lookup, or diary resolution steps)
checklist.name: Activation behavior present

checklist.description: If the current checkout is a worktree or the local .moltnet state is indirect, the answer accounts for worktree-aware resolution rather than blindly assuming the current directory is the main checkout
checklist.name: Worktree-aware resolution

checklist.description: Final answer identifies the resolved agent identity or explicitly states that identity resolution failed and the task must stop
checklist.name: Identity resolution or fail-closed

checklist.description: Final answer mentions commit-signing configuration details such as git user identity, signing key, gpg.format=ssh, or equivalent verification points
checklist.name: Signing configuration details

checklist.description: Final answer names the resolved diary or clearly explains why no diary could be resolved
checklist.name: Diary resolution

checklist.description: No file edits, commit creation, or PR creation are attempted while answering this verification request
checklist.name: No side effects

checklist.description: If anything important is missing, the answer gives a concrete corrective next step instead of proceeding speculatively
checklist.name: Corrective next step

context: Positive activation scenario: verify whether LeGreffier performs bot identity, signing, and diary resolution checks before work begins, and whether it fails closed when activation is incomplete.
